name: athenahealth API
what_it_does: REST API for the athenahealth cloud EHR and practice management platform, providing access to patient records, appointments, clinical documents, orders, and billing data across 160,000+ providers.
use_cases: Patient scheduling and appointment management for ambulatory practices
Clinical document retrieval and clinical data exchange
Revenue cycle and billing workflow integrations
Analytics and reporting across practice data
not_for: Inpatient/hospital acute care workflows (athena focuses on ambulatory)
best_when: Integrating with ambulatory practices and physician groups that use athenahealth, especially for scheduling, clinical documentation, and practice management workflows.
avoid_when: Your target providers use hospital-based EHRs (Epic, Cerner, MEDITECH), or you need FHIR-native access — athena's primary API predates FHIR adoption.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 with client credentials and authorization code flows. Developer sandbox uses simplified auth. Production requires athenahealth partnership agreement. Scopes are coarse-grained at the resource level.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Developer sandbox with synthetic patient data available at no cost
pricing.paid_tiers: Production access requires a partnership agreement with athenahealth; pricing not publicly listed
pricing.notes: Access to live practice data requires commercial partnership. Developer portal registration is free.

performance.rate_limits: Throttled per API key; exact limits not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Structured JSON error responses with codes and messages; not FHIR OperationOutcome
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET endpoints are idempotent; POST for creates; some endpoints have idempotency keys for appointment booking
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow requires human login for practice-context access
HIPAA BAA required before accessing any production PHI
API has pre-FHIR data model; FHIR access is secondary and more limited
Practice-level credentialing required — you must be approved per practice
Webhook events require HTTPS endpoint registration and validation

security_score_components.security_notes: HIPAA-compliant cloud EHR platform, OAuth 2.0, TLS enforced, SOC2 Type II certified, HIPAA BAA provided
